management_notes:
Philippe Cloutier is the founder, President, and CEO with 35+ years of Abitibi exploration experience including discovery credit for the Bell-Allard South Cu-Zn Mine. He founded Cartier Resources in 2006 and has maintained active operational leadership through two full market cycles — rare continuity for a TSXV junior. In January 2025, Cloutier personally purchased 200,000 shares on the open market at C$0.10/share, a C$20,000 commitment that is modest in dollar terms but meaningful as a signal of management's confidence at the time of the company's capital floor.

Agnico Eagle Mines holds approximately 27.7-28% of Cartier's shares (117,792,944 shares as of March 2025) and has made multiple strategic investments over several years, most recently a C$2.7M investment at C$0.13/unit in March 2025. Agnico also participates in a technical committee — their geologists have direct access to the exploration data and understand the deposit geology intimately. This level of engagement from one of the world's premier gold miners, with three mills in the Val-d'Or region and historical familiarity with the Larder Lake-Cadillac Fault Zone (LLCFZ), is the single most important management validation signal in this scorecard.

Share dilution of approximately 22.3% over the past year reflects aggressive capital raising to fund the 100,000-metre drill program — appropriate for the scale of the exploration campaign but worth monitoring for sustained dilution pressure.
geology_notes:
The Cadillac Project (100%-owned, 15 km of strike along the Larder Lake-Cadillac Fault Zone in Quebec) hosts a February 2025 NI 43-101 Mineral Resource: 767,800 oz measured and indicated at 2.40 g/t Au (9,953,000 tonnes) and 2,416,900 oz inferred at 2.14 g/t Au (35,185,000 tonnes), totalling approximately 3.18 Moz. The 48% increase in inferred ounces from the prior estimate reflects the impact of the expanding drill program. The resource includes the past-producing Chimo Mine (379,012 oz Au historically produced 1964-1997) and multiple parallel gold zones along the LLCFZ.

The March 2026 Hope Target discovery is a significant geological development: drill holes CA26-327 returned 5.2 g/t Au over 5.0 m and 1.2 g/t Au over 25.5 m in Pontiac sediments, interpreted as a new intrusion-related (porphyritic) gold system distinct from the main LLCFZ shear-hosted mineralisation. A new deposit style on the same property expands the geological model and opens targets that have not been previously drill-tested. Recent high-grade results from the Contact Zone (16.7 g/t Au over 2.1 m with visible gold grains) and Portal Target (7.1 g/t Au over 8.0 m) demonstrate continued high-grade potential throughout the system.

The 100,000-metre, 18-month, approximately 600-hole drill program (commenced late August 2025) is the largest ever at Cadillac and is generating frequent news releases. A database of 4,477 drill holes totalling 535,360 metres from Cartier plus prior operators underpins the resource model. Agnico Eagle operates three mills within toll-milling distance — a potential production pathway that would not require greenfield mill construction.
capital_notes:
Cartier Resources has approximately 446 million basic shares outstanding with approximately C$11M in cash (described as 'fully funded' for the 100,000-metre program per Crux Investor). The company is debt-free. The April 2025 C$11.4M private placement via Paradigm Capital was the largest single financing in the company's history, demonstrating institutional appetite for the Cadillac project story. March 2025 saw the Agnico Eagle strategic investment at C$0.13/unit.

The 22.3% year-on-year share dilution is the primary capital structure concern — at 446 million shares outstanding, the share count is large relative to the company's stage and limits the per-share value accretion from resource growth. Multiple warrant tranches from various financing rounds add to the potential fully diluted count (estimated 480-520 million, unconfirmed). The high share count reflects the company's history of financing exploration through periods of depressed gold prices.

The 100,000-metre drill program is explicitly funded within the current treasury. No resource update, PEA, or feasibility study timeline has been announced — meaning the company will likely require another capital raise in 2026 or 2027 when the drill program concludes and before economic studies commence.
catalyst_notes:
The active 100,000-metre, 2-rig drill program (approximately 600 holes, running through mid-2027) generates frequent news releases and is the primary near-term news driver. Results are being released on an ongoing basis from multiple zones (Contact, Portal, Hope Target, others) simultaneously, providing regular investor touchpoints. The March 2026 Hope Target discovery (new intrusion-related gold system, 5.2 g/t over 5.0 m) added a new geological dimension that could attract attention from geologists and technical investors assessing the district-scale potential.

A resource update is expected at some point during or after the 100,000-metre program — the previous MRE showed a 48% increase in inferred ounces. If the ongoing program continues to expand the inferred resource, a future MRE could approach 4+ Moz total, which would cross an important psychological threshold for potential acquirers and institutional investors. Agnico Eagle's annual strategic investment review is an ongoing implicit catalyst.

The Probe Resources/Fresnillo (C$780M) and Northern Superior/IAMGOLD (C$267M) acquisitions in the Abitibi validate continued major-producer appetite for district-scale Quebec gold projects — comparable transactions that would translate to significant premiums above Cartier's current C$103M market cap if Agnico or a competing acquirer formalises interest.
acquisition_notes:
Cartier trades at an enterprise value of approximately C$92M, implying approximately US$21/oz on total resource (3.18 Moz M+I+Inferred) and approximately US$113/oz on M+I only (767,800 oz). The total-resource metric of US$21/oz is cheap but appropriately reflects that 76% of the ounces are inferred (not measured or indicated) and no PEA has been completed. The M+I-only metric of US$113/oz is more comparable to peer transactions and suggests reasonable value.

Agnico Eagle's 27.7-28% strategic stake, combined with their three operating mills within toll-milling distance of Cadillac and their technical committee participation, positions them as the most probable eventual acquirer. The Probe/Fresnillo transaction at approximately US$55/oz total resource (Fresnillo valued Probe's ~10 Moz at approximately C$78/oz) and the IAMGOLD/Northern Superior transaction at approximately US$111/oz M+I are the most relevant comparables — both in the Abitibi region.

The Hope Target intrusion-related discovery adds a new dimension of acquisition value that would not typically be priced in standard per-ounce resource metrics. If confirmed at meaningful scale, it could expand the resource to 5+ Moz total and attract competing bidder interest beyond Agnico.
analyst_summary:
WATCH (19/25). Cartier Resources is the closest thing to a validated Abitibi gold acquisition target at current stage — Agnico Eagle's 28% stake, technical committee involvement, and proximity of three operating mills make a future acquisition the most logical corporate outcome once the resource reaches economic scale. The 100,000-metre drill program (in progress through mid-2027) is generating frequent high-grade results and a growing resource: 3.18 Moz total at 2.14-2.40 g/t Au with multiple zones still open along the 15 km LLCFZ strike.

The primary concerns are the large share count (446M shares, 22.3% dilution past year) and the absence of a PEA or feasibility study timeline — the company is still in the resource-building phase with no clear path to a production decision. The 76% inferred portion of the resource is appropriate for this stage but limits valuation credibility for institutional investors requiring measured and indicated ounces. Cash of C$11M is adequate for the funded drill program but will require replenishment before economic studies can commence.

The key catalyst to watch is the Hope Target follow-up drilling results in 2026. If the new intrusion-related gold system at Hope grows to 1+ Moz inferred in a future MRE, it would add a distinct deposit style to the Cadillac project and likely trigger a formal Agnico Eagle strategic review. The combination of established LLCFZ resources plus a new porphyry-related discovery on the same 100%-owned property would be a compelling case for consolidation.
